scenario_label: Cold outreach — ML engineer at a Series B AI infra company

input.recipient_context: Senior ML Engineer at Vellum. LinkedIn bio: 'Building reliable LLM evaluation tooling. Previously at Scale AI.' Top GitHub repos: vellum-eval (1.2k stars, RAG eval harness), prompt-bench (340 stars). Specialization: LLM evals, RAG. Experience level: senior.
input.instructions: Write a concise warm cold email. Anchor the opening on the recipient's eval/RAG work, not on Vellum the product. Pitch: I've shipped production RAG pipelines + eval harnesses (10+ yrs SWE, 4 yrs LLM). One CTA: 30-min intro chat next week.

must_avoid_phrases: I hope this finds you well

scenario_label: Follow-up after job application — no response 2 weeks

input.recipient_context: Engineering Manager, Frontend Platform at Linear. Bio: 'Shipping the fastest issue tracker on the web.' Job applied: Senior Frontend Engineer, applied 2026-04-15, status: no response.
input.instructions: Follow-up email. Applied for Senior Frontend Engineer 2 weeks ago, no response. Reaffirm interest, mention Next.js + TypeScript + perf experience overlap with Linear's stack. CTA: ask about timeline.

expected_signals: applied 2 weeks ago / earlier this month
Next.js or TypeScript or frontend perf
must_avoid_phrases: I hope you are doing well
I trust this email finds you

input.recipient_context: PhD candidate at Stanford NLP. First-author on 'Selective Self-Refinement for Reasoning LLMs' (NeurIPS 2025, 142 cites). Specialization: reasoning, self-correction, LLM training.
input.instructions: Reference the Selective Self-Refinement paper specifically (the selective-correction angle, not generic praise). Note: I built a prod RAG pipeline that uses a similar self-critique step. Ask if she'd be open to a 20-min call comparing findings.

expected_signals: Selective Self-Refinement (paper title) or self-correction / self-critique
must_avoid_phrases: I hope this email finds you well

scenario_label: Cold outreach — founder of a small devtool company

input.recipient_context: Founder/CTO at Ping.gg. Bio: 'Live-stream collaboration tools for creators.' Tech stack: Next.js, tRPC, Vercel. Active OSS contributor (create-t3-app).
input.instructions: Cold email. Anchor opening on Ping.gg's Next.js/tRPC stack overlap with my background. Don't mention create-t3-app generically — reference live-stream collaboration use case. CTA: explore freelance/contract work.

must_avoid_phrases: I hope this finds you well

scenario_label: Cold outreach — engineer with notable OSS repo

input.recipient_context: Staff Engineer at Replicate. Top GitHub repo: cog-runtime (8.4k stars — container runtime for ML models). Bio: 'Making ML models easy to ship.' Languages: Go, Python, Rust.
input.instructions: Cold email. Reference cog-runtime by name and mention the Rust + Cloudflare Workers angle of my background as relevant. CTA: 25-min chat about how Replicate handles edge inference.

must_avoid_phrases: I trust this email finds you

scenario_label: Outreach triggered by a recent LinkedIn post

input.recipient_context: Engineering Lead at Zed Industries. Bio: 'Code editor built in Rust, GPU-accelerated UI.' Languages: Rust, GPUI.
input.linkedin_post_content: We just shipped multi-buffer collaborative editing in Zed. Spent 6 months on conflict-free CRDT replication. The single hardest engineering problem of my career. Open-sourced the protocol spec — link in comments.
input.instructions: Reference the multi-buffer CRDT post specifically (the protocol-spec open-sourcing, not the editor generally). I've shipped real-time collab features in WebSocket apps and have Rust/Wasm experience. CTA: 20-min call to discuss the spec.

expected_signals: CRDT or multi-buffer or collaborative editing
must_avoid_phrases: I hope this finds you well
